stories_covered: Mary Meeker AI report: infrastructure spend, token cost collapse, China AI threat
OpenAI growth projections vs Google 20 years ago
Next 32% of world coming online as AI-first generation
harry_takes: TVPI has signal as proxy for intermediate performance but DPI is ultimate metric; Chamath's statement extreme but directionally right
1 billion dollar fund is the 'death zone' for venture; profitable at 200M or 10B+, but unsustainable at 500M-1B
Hyperscalers have become CapEx hogs; 2 trillion in infrastructure spend ahead of application revenue, sustainable only if growth continues
Existential dread missing from B2B startups; incumbents can survive poor execution but startups cannot; AI demands speed (ship Wednesdays, not Q4)
Next generation won't use legacy SaaS UIs; files disappearing, AI agents will displace systems of record
harry_boldest_quote: Public company investors are just mean VCs on steroids.
predictions: Lessin: Only two fund sizes survive: 200M seed-focused or 10B+ mega-funds; 500M-1B tier hollowed out, can't compete
Panel: If OpenAI misses growth by 30-40%, 'scary moment' arrives; expectations so high anything less than 'amazing' feels like failure
Stebbings: Token costs will continue 99.7% collapse; any B2B founder claiming 'too expensive' misunderstands trajectory
Lemkin: AI slow-roll (limited release Q4, expand 2026) will kill B2B companies; must go all-in like Facebook post-IPO mobile pivot
disagreements: Lemkin dismisses 'anything not important doesn't matter' as nihilism; argues solid 5-10B companies merit respect. O'Driscoll defends mid-market funds, argues pricing discipline harder at late stage. Lessin argues TVPI has zero signal; Rory counters with ML analysis showing correlation to outcomes.
harry_role: Harry moderates spirited debate with Lessin early; challenges both Lemkin and O'Driscoll positions. Drives conversation toward B2B startup AI adoption urgency. Acts as provocateur on whether non-100B companies 'matter' while also defending mid-market investor legitimacy.
notable: Heated debate on fund materiality and whether achieving 5-10B outcomes 'matters'; China AI threat underestimated (93% OpenAI O3-mini performance at 0.2% cost via Alibaba); next generation treating legacy SaaS interfaces as alien technology.
